channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump names pick for ambassador to Japan
item.description: President-elect Trump on Monday named George Edward Glass as his pick for ambassador to Japan. “I am pleased to announce George Edward Glass as our next United States Ambassador to Japan,” Trump said in his Truth Social post.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: France rushes aid to Mayotte after Cyclone Chido leaves hundreds feared dead
item.description: MAMOUDZOU, Mayotte (AP) — France used ships and military aircraft to rush rescuers and supplies to Mayotte on Monday after the tiny French island territory off Africa was battered by its worst cyclone in nearly a century. Authorities fear hundreds and possibly thousands of people have died.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, THOMAS ADAMSON and RAINAT ALILOIFFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN announces initiative to overcome political deadlock in Libya
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The top U.N. official in Libya announced a new initiative Monday to overcome a three-year deadlock and move the divided oil-rich North African nation toward a national election. The U.N. deputy special envoy for Libya, Stephanie Koury, told the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Negotiators say Israel and Hamas are inching toward a ceasefire deal. This is what it may look like
item.description: After months of deadlock, Israel and Hamas appear to be moving closer toward a ceasefire to end their 14-month war. Top officials from the U.S., Qatar and Egypt have resumed their mediation efforts in recent weeks and reported greater willingness by the warring sides to wrap up a deal.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukraine and US say some North Korean troops have been killed fighting alongside Russian forces
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s military intelligence agency and the Pentagon said Monday that some North Korean troops have been killed during combat against Ukrainian forces in Russia’s Kursk border region. These are the first reported casualties since the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and TARA COPP, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says Turkey ‘did an unfriendly takeover’ in Syria as US-brokered cease-fire appears to fail
item.description: President-elect Trump described the fall of the Assad regime in Syria as an "unfriendly takeover" by neighboring Turkey as sources tell Fox News Digital the U.S.-brokered cease-fire has collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 26 people arrested in crackdown on illegal deforestation along Paraguay, Brazil and Argentina border
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Paraguayan authorities arrested 26 people in a crackdown on suspected illegal deforestation along the country's border with Brazil and Argentina, Interpol announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: German Chancellor Olaf Scholz loses no-confidence vote, election called for early next year
item.description: In November, Scholz's government collapsed after the Free Democrat Party quit over the country's debt. An early election to replace Scholz will occur on Feb. 23 following Monday's no-confidence vote.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian finance minister notes Trump tariff threat in resignation letter
item.description: Canada's finance minister noted President-elect Trump’s tariff threat in her resignation letter dated Monday. “Our country today faces a grave challenge,” Chrystia Freeland said in a letter addressed to Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump says Russia-Ukraine peace may be harder than Middle East
item.description: President-elect Trump on Monday expressed confidence that he could settle the war between Russia and Ukraine but also stressed that it would be difficult and possibly harder to solve than the conflict between Israel and the Palestinian militant group Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Germany's Scholz loses a confidence vote, setting up an early election in February
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz lost a confidence vote in the German parliament on Monday, putting the European Union’s most populous member and biggest economy on course to hold an early election in February.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan rights group reports 3rd in-custody death among people detained in post-election unrest
item.description: A man detained in Venezuela during the civil unrest that followed the country’s disputed July presidential election has died while in custody.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine details North Korean casualties in Russia's Kursk region
item.description: Ukraine is reporting at least 30 North Korean soldiers were killed or wounded over the weekend around several villages on the front in Russia's Kursk region.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: EU slaps sanctions on dozens more Russian officials and targets its shadow oil and gas fleet
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The European Union on Monday imposed fresh sanctions on dozens of officials linked to Russia’s war on Ukraine, including two top North Korean defense officials, a military unit blamed for an attack on a Kyiv children’s hospital and the heads of companies in the energy sector.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU seeks assurances from Syria's new leaders in exchange for dropping sanctions
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union nations on Monday set out conditions for lifting sanctions on Syria and kick-starting aid to the conflict-ravaged country amid uncertainty about its new leaders' intentions just over a week after they seized power.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Filipina who won a last-minute reprieve from Indonesian firing squad prepares to fly home
item.description: YOGYAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — A Filipina who was on death row in Indonesia and was nearly executed by firing squad in 2015 will return home this week under an arrangement between the countries, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: SLAMET RIYADI and NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel will close its Ireland embassy over Gaza tensions as Palestinian death toll nears 45,000
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel said Sunday it will close its embassy in Ireland as relations deteriorated over the war in Gaza, where Palestinian medical officials said new Israeli airstrikes killed over 46 people including several children.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Death toll in French territory of Mayotte from Cyclone Chido is 'several hundred,' top official says
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — The death toll in the French territory of Mayotte from Cyclone Chido is “several hundred” and may run into the thousands, the island’s top government official told a local broadcaster Sunday.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Chronicling the Assad regime from death of the father to defeat of the son
item.description: Fox News senior foreign affairs correspondent Greg Palkot documents his years of reporting from Syria in the aftermath of the fall of the regime of Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Christian leader in Lebanon urges US, allies to intervene to stop Hezbollah
item.description: The leader of a Christian coalition and political party in Lebanon is calling on the U.S. and its Western allies to deploy troops to Lebanon to help the army dismantle Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea’s president impeached over brief martial law decree
item.description: South Korea's parliament voted to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol early Saturday morning, after a brief decree of martial law thrust the country into temporary political peril. The motion to impeach Yoon passed with a 204-85 vote, The Associated Press (AP) reported.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: UN talks fail to reach agreement on dealing with rising risk of global drought
item.description: BENGALURU, India (AP) — Despite two weeks of U.N.-sponsored talks in Saudi Arabia’s Riyadh, the participating 197 nations failed to agree early Saturday on a plan to deal with global droughts, made longer and more severe by a warming climate.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Woman arrested smuggling pounds of meth wrapped as a Christmas gift
item.description: Customs officers arrested a traveler who investigators say tried to smuggle pounds of methamphetamine disguised as a Christmas gift into New Zealand last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Saudi Arabia's plans to host the men's World Cup 2034 will be harmful for the climate, experts say
item.description: As the newly-named host of the 2034 World Cup in men's soccer, Saudi Arabia says it will construct or renovate 15 stadiums, create a futuristic city and expand airports in a massive buildout to accommodate millions of athletes, coaches and spectators.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US military flies American released from Syrian prison to Jordan, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has transported out of Syria an American who had disappeared seven months ago into former President Bashar Assad’s notorious prison system and was among the thousands released this week by rebels, U.S. officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US military flies missing American out of Syria
item.description: The U.S. military on Friday flew a missing American citizen out of Syria this week, after the stunning fall of Syrian leader Bashar Assad's regime. Travis Timmerman was transferred to U.S. forces in Syria on Friday, according to a defense official, and was flown to Jordan. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: France's Macron names centrist ally Bayrou as next prime minister
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron named centrist ally François Bayrou as prime minister, after a historic parliamentary vote ousted the previous government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top US ally, SDF commander in Syria warns of ISIS return if Turkish airstrikes don’t stop
item.description: Gen. Mazloum Abdi, the commander of the Syrian Democratic Forces, said the Turkish military continue to attack his Kurdish forces, despite a U.S. brokered ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trudeau declares himself ‘proud feminist’ after lamenting Harris loss to Trump as setback for women
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau on Tuesday declared himself a "proud feminist" as he lamented Vice President Harris’ loss to President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US troops to remain in Syria, White House says
item.description: The ouster of Bashar al-Assad has left Biden’s outgoing administration to maneuver through a volatile moment in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: 1,500-year-old Ten Commandments tablet set for auction
item.description: The tablet has twenty lines of text but only nine of the commandments, with "Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord in vain" omitted.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Journalists' rights group says 104 media workers killed this year
item.description: More than half of the journalists and media workers killed perished during the Israel-Hamas war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Here's how the US is countering the Islamic State group during Syria's upheaval
item.description: The U.S. has had troops in Syria for the last decade to battle IS. The tumult following a rebel offensive that toppled Assad has raised fears of an Islamic State resurgence.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, NewsNation
